tossups.question: An artist with this first name painted a possible political allegory in which an approaching dog in the lower-left corner is repelled by a large white swan. This is the first name of an artist whose careers as a brewer and innkeeper influenced paintings like Beware of Luxury and The Dancing Couple. That artist with this first name painted rowdy genre scenes like The Feast of Saint Nicholas and inspired two “interiors” in a series by Joan Miró. Many of the 34 paintings attributed to an artist with this first name are set in a room of his home with checkered floors and a shining window on the left. Those paintings include one in which a foot warmer sits on the floor behind a domestic worker as she pours the title liquid. For 10 points, give this first name of the Dutch Golden Age painter of The Milkmaid.
tossups.question_sanitized: An artist with this first name painted a possible political allegory in which an approaching dog in the lower-left corner is repelled by a large white swan. This is the first name of an artist whose careers as a brewer and innkeeper influenced paintings like Beware of Luxury and The Dancing Couple. That artist with this first name painted rowdy genre scenes like The Feast of Saint Nicholas and inspired two "interiors" in a series by Joan Miro. Many of the 34 paintings attributed to an artist with this first name are set in a room of his home with checkered floors and a shining window on the left. Those paintings include one in which a foot warmer sits on the floor behind a domestic worker as she pours the title liquid. For 10 points, give this first name of the Dutch Golden Age painter of The Milkmaid.
tossups.answer: Jan (“yahn”) [or Johannes; accept Jan Asselijn or Jan Steen or Jan Vermeer or Johannes Vermeer] (The first painting is The Threatened Swan by Jan Asselijn. Miró’s series is Dutch Interiors.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Jan ("yahn") [or Johannes; accept Jan Asselijn or Jan Steen or Jan Vermeer or Johannes Vermeer] (The first painting is The Threatened Swan by Jan Asselijn. Miro's series is Dutch Interiors.)

tossups.question: A painting by this artist shows a woman balancing a glass of wine in the lap of a man whose unusually positioned legs reverse the proverb “it takes strong legs to carry wealth,” and is called Beware of Luxury. This artist painted multiple theater-inspired scenes of a doctor clothed in dramatic costume visiting lovesick or pregnant young women. One of this artist’s paintings contrasts a boy crying over his lack of presents with his sister delighted by her gift of a doll on the title holiday. That painting, along with another by this artist of a large family drinking, smoking, and playing instruments, is displayed in the Gallery of Honour at the Rijksmuseum (“RIKES-museum”), as is a work in which children teach a cat to dance. For 10 points, name this 17th-century Dutch painter of lively genre scenes like The Feast of St. Nicholas and The Merry Family.
tossups.question_sanitized: A painting by this artist shows a woman balancing a glass of wine in the lap of a man whose unusually positioned legs reverse the proverb "it takes strong legs to carry wealth," and is called Beware of Luxury. This artist painted multiple theater-inspired scenes of a doctor clothed in dramatic costume visiting lovesick or pregnant young women. One of this artist's paintings contrasts a boy crying over his lack of presents with his sister delighted by her gift of a doll on the title holiday. That painting, along with another by this artist of a large family drinking, smoking, and playing instruments, is displayed in the Gallery of Honour at the Rijksmuseum ("RIKES-museum"), as is a work in which children teach a cat to dance. For 10 points, name this 17th-century Dutch painter of lively genre scenes like The Feast of St. Nicholas and The Merry Family.

tossups.question: This activity is depicted on the second card of a Floskaartjes (“FLOSS-kar-chiss”) deck, before 34 cards representing social roles. A “set” for doing this activity was placed beside a lunar map and an egg in the first shadow box by Joseph Cornell. In “The Primacy of Absorption,” Michael Fried points out a torn jacket in a painting of a person performing this activity. This activity is shown in a painting that John Everett Millais used to (*) advertise the company Pears. Manet painted teenage Léon Leenhoff doing this activity while holding a bowl. This activity symbolized impermanence in the homo bulla motif of 17th-century Dutch painting. Unlike a painting in which a house of cards is being built, another painting by the same artist shows a young man using a straw to do this action. For 10 points, Chardin painted a young man leaning out of a window while doing what action?
tossups.question_sanitized: This activity is depicted on the second card of a Floskaartjes ("FLOSS-kar-chiss") deck, before 34 cards representing social roles. A "set" for doing this activity was placed beside a lunar map and an egg in the first shadow box by Joseph Cornell. In "The Primacy of Absorption," Michael Fried points out a torn jacket in a painting of a person performing this activity. This activity is shown in a painting that John Everett Millais used to (*) advertise the company Pears. Manet painted teenage Leon Leenhoff doing this activity while holding a bowl. This activity symbolized impermanence in the homo bulla motif of 17th-century Dutch painting. Unlike a painting in which a house of cards is being built, another painting by the same artist shows a young man using a straw to do this action. For 10 points, Chardin painted a young man leaning out of a window while doing what action?
tossups.answer: blowing bubbles [or blowing soap bubbles; accept making bubbles] (Life of Man is by Jan Steen.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: blowing bubbles [or blowing soap bubbles; accept making bubbles] (Life of Man is by Jan Steen.)

tossups.question: A painting by this artist depicts a flirtatious man next to a virgin playing the harpsichord, which is inscribed with the title ironic phrase Acta Virum Probant, meaning “actions prove the man.” In another of his paintings, broken egg shells near two women indicate the then well-known sexual euphemism of “cracking eggs in a pan.” A teacher uses the plaster nude on his desk to instruct a child and young woman in a third of his paintings. This artist of The Christening Feast and The Drawing Lesson painted multiple tavern scenes inspired by his profession as an innkeeper. His characteristic paintings include one in which mischievous children teach a cat how to dance, and one in which a child in a gold dress has received all the gifts during the title celebration. For 10 points, name this painter of interior comedy scenes, such as The Feast of St. Nicholas.

packet.name: Round 05 - Rice, Illinois, and William _ Mary

A teacher uses the plaster nude on his desk to instruct a child and young woman in a third of his paintings. This artist of The Christening Feast and The Drawing Lesson painted multiple tavern scenes inspired by his profession as an innkeeper. His characteristic paintings include one in which mischievous children teach a cat how to dance, and one in which a child in a gold dress has received all the gifts during the title celebration. bonuses.leadin: The decline of this country’s “Golden Age” may have begun with its rampjaar, or “disaster year,” in 1672. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this modern-day country home to the Delft school of painters. The wife of Jan Vermeer, an artist from this country, claimed that the rampjaar was so bad that her husband was unable to sell any paintings afterwards.
The collapse of the Dutch art market drove this other Golden Age painter to open a tavern. His often-humorous paintings include Rhetoricians at a Window and Children Teaching a Cat to Dance.

Steen co-founded a guild dedicated to this saint in Leiden. The many guilds dedicated to this patron saint of artists provided training for a fee and tried to keep prices high by restricting the production and sale of art.
answers_sanitized: Holland [or the Netherlands; prompt on the Dutch Republic]
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: The decline of this country's "Golden Age" may have begun with its rampjaar, or "disaster year," in 1672. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: Name this modern-day country home to the Delft school of painters. The wife of Jan Vermeer, an artist from this country, claimed that the rampjaar was so bad that her husband was unable to sell any paintings afterwards.
The collapse of the Dutch art market drove this other Golden Age painter to open a tavern. His often-humorous paintings include Rhetoricians at a Window and Children Teaching a Cat to Dance.

Steen co-founded a guild dedicated to this saint in Leiden. The many guilds dedicated to this patron saint of artists provided training for a fee and tried to keep prices high by restricting the production and sale of art.

bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions about Dutch Renaissance artists. For 10 points each:
parts: This painter depicted several young and old people playing instruments around a dinner table in The Merry Family. His other family scenes include The Drawing Lesson and The Dancing Couple.
The artist Frans Hals is best known for this painting, which shows a soldier in full costume with an enigmatic smile.
This man painted a self-portrait in a red turban and the Madonna of Chancellor Rolin, but is better known for The Arnolfini Wedding.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer some questions about Dutch Renaissance artists. For 10 points each:
parts_sanitized: This painter depicted several young and old people playing instruments around a dinner table in The Merry Family. His other family scenes include The Drawing Lesson and The Dancing Couple.
The artist Frans Hals is best known for this painting, which shows a soldier in full costume with an enigmatic smile.
This man painted a self-portrait in a red turban and the Madonna of Chancellor Rolin, but is better known for The Arnolfini Wedding.

bonuses.leadin: An owl sits on the shoulder of a woman holding a pewter mug in this artist's painting, Malle Babbe. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist, also known for his group portraiture, such as his paintings of the Regents and Regentesses of the Old Men's Almshouse. His The Laughing Cavalier depicts a man smiling, not laughing.
Born in Antwerp, Frans Hals moved to Haarlem in this modern-day country during its Golden Age. Jan Vermeer painted a view of Delft, a city in this country, in 1660.
This other artist of the Dutch Golden Age painted lively family scenes, such as The Feast of Saint Nicholas in which a boy is crying because the only gift he's received is a lousy birch rod.
answers_sanitized: Frans Hals [or Frans Hals the Elder]
The Netherlands [or the Dutch Republic; or Holland]
Jan Steen (yahn SHTAIN) [or Jan Havickszoon Steen]

bonuses.leadin: One figure in the background of this painting holds a child in his right arm and gestures upwards with his other hand, and in the foreground appears a basket of various breads. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this painting in which a golden haired girl carries a doll and a basket of treats while her older brother sobs on her right.
This Dutch artist of The Merry Family painted The Feast of St. Nicholas as well as a depiction of Adolf and Catharina Croeser, a wealthy merchant of Delft and his daughter.
Steen was a contemporary of this other Dutch genre painter whose well known works include The Milkmaid, Girl With a Pearl Earring, and The Art of Painting.

This Dutch artist of The Merry Family painted The Feast of St. Nicholas as well as a depiction of Adolf and Catharina Croeser, a wealthy merchant of Delft and his daughter.
Steen was a contemporary of this other Dutch genre painter whose well known works include The Milkmaid, Girl With a Pearl Earring, and The Art of Painting.

bonuses.leadin: Five men in black hats look past the viewer as a hatless man stands behind them in this painter’s Syndics of the Draper’s Guild. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this artist who painted a kneeling man being forgiven by his father in Return of the Prodigal Son, as well as Aristotle Contemplating the Bust of Homer.
This other Dutch artist painted two similar scenes of a woman removing her sock, Woman at Her Toilet and The Morning Toilet. He painted a man holding a fiddle and raising a glass in The Merry Family.

In this Steen painting, a boy in a brown hat cries on the left as a yellow-dressed girl in the middle holds a doll and a bucket of sweets. It depicts a scene of domestic gift-giving during the title celebration.

bonuses.leadin: Answer some questions about the Dutch Golden Age and paintings created during that time period, for 10 points each:
parts: One of the most illustrious painters is this man from Delft whose famous paintings include The Milkmaid, Girl With a Pearl Earring and The Little Street.
Major works by this portraitist from Haarlem include Laughing Cavalier and Banquet of the Officers of the St George Militia Company.
This artist created many moralizing paintings of out-of-control households, such as The Drawing Lesson and In Luxury, Look Out!
